generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance JointEncoderSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-resolution encoder for capturing joint positional data with millisecond precision for robot dynamics calibration"
    DTDFVocab:producedData JointPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance ForceTorqueSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-fidelity force/torque sensor for capturing nonlinear friction dynamics at robot joints with kHz sampling rate"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ForceTorqueDataTransmitted
]

instance RobotStateMonitor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "State monitoring device capturing full robot state including positional accuracy and dynamic load profiles for adaptive control"
    DTDFVocab:producedData RobotStateDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance JointPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom JointEncoderSensor
]

instance ForceTorqueDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ForceTorqueSensor
]

instance RobotStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom RobotStateMonitor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PhysicsBasedRobotModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box Simulink-based dynamic simulation model for interpretable robot dynamics with explainable control logic"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkExecutionEngine
]

instance NonlinearFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Black-box machine learning model capturing nonlinear friction phenomena not easily representable analytically"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MachineLearningProcessingEngine
]

instance RobotGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric representation implicitly referenced via SysML or AADL/MontiArc for structural system composition"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelConverter
]

instance RobotBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "State machine model in SysML v1.7 or MontiArc Automaton describing system dynamics at logical/functional levels"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcSimulationEngine
]

instance ContextualDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces from Digital Shadow Reference Model for purpose-specific manufacturing process abstraction"
    DTDFVocab:fromData RobotStateDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationFramework
]

instance ProcessParameterData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time sensor streams including temperature, pressure, and cycle time parameters for injection molding optimization"
    DTDFVocab:fromData JointPositionDataTransmitted, ForceTorqueDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ProcessOptimizationEngine
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MQTTCommunicationLayer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol via Mosquitto for lightweight publish-subscribe communication between edge devices and central DT components"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance ThingsBoardPlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "IoT platform for data ingestion, normalization, and routing of heterogeneous sensor data to DT components"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataProcessingService
]

instance SimulinkExecutionEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink engine for executing physics-based robot dynamics simulations and control logic"
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationValidationService
]

instance MachineLearningProcessingEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom ML processing engine for executing black-box models of nonlinear friction phenomena"
    DTDFVocab:enables AnomalyDetectionService
]

instance SysMLModelConverter : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-to-simulation mapping framework with traceability mechanisms for model transformations"
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelIntegrationService
]

instance MontiArcSimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc framework for executing state machine-based behavioral models of robot systems"
    DTDFVocab:enables BehavioralValidationService
]

instance DataAggregationFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital Shadow framework for aggregating and abstracting raw sensor data into purpose-driven contextual traces"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAbstractionService
]

instance ProcessOptimizationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Closed-loop optimization engine for adjusting injection molding parameters based on DT insights"
    DTDFVocab:enables ProcessOptimizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Passive data collection service aggregating real-time sensor streams for digital shadow representation"
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalStateInsight
]

instance ProcessOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Decision support service for continuous process optimization through parameter adjustments"
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendationAction
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive service identifying deviations from nominal operational parameters in manufacturing processes"
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyAlertInsight
]

instance DataProcessingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for normalizing and routing heterogeneous sensor data to DT components"
    DTDFVocab:provides ProcessContextInsight
]

instance DataAbstractionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for aggregating raw sensor data into purpose-driven contextual traces with noise reduction"
    DTDFVocab:provides ProcessContextInsight
]

instance SimulationValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service validating digital twin models against physical system behaviors through simulation"
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelValidationInsight
]

instance ModelIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service ensuring traceability and correctness of model transformations between SysML and simulation tools"
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelIntegrationInsight
]

instance BehavioralValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service validating state machine-based behavioral models against physical system behaviors"
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelValidationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance OperationalStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time operational state of manufacturing processes derived from aggregated sensor data traces"
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated recommendation for adjusting process parameters to optimize production efficiency"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AnomalyAlertInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified anomalies in manufacturing processes based on probabilistic latent variable analysis"
]

instance ProcessContextInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Contextualized process data derived from aggregated sensor streams for specific manufacturing purposes"
]

instance ModelValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation results comparing digital twin model predictions with physical system behaviors"
]

instance ModelIntegrationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Traceability information ensuring correctness of model transformations between SysML and simulation components"
]

// C1: Environment
instance ManufacturingDigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains RobotPhysicalSystem, DigitalTwinCore, ManufacturingExecutionSystem
]

instance RobotPhysicalSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical robot system including actuators, sensors, and end-effectors for injection molding operations"
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingDigitalTwinEnvironment
]

instance DigitalTwinCore : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Core digital twin components including models, data processing engines, and service layers"
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingDigitalTwinEnvironment
]

instance ManufacturingExecutionSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Manufacturing execution system integrating DT insights with production planning and control systems"
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingDigitalTwinEnvironment
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system described in the documents emphasizes **temporal synchronization** between physical and digital components through **data trace aggregation and abstraction**, where data flows are processed in near-real-time to maintain consistency. The **temporal granularity** is determined by the **frequency of data trace collection** from the cyber-physical system (CPS), which is critical for decision support in continuous processes (e.g., injection molding). While explicit synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz, ms) are not detailed, the system relies on **fine-grained traceability** of data traces—where each trace originates from a processing component and is linked to input data, models, and computational steps. Latency requirements are implicitly addressed via **processing components** that compute higher-level abstractions from raw data traces, ensuring that temporal dependencies are preserved in transitive traceability chains. The **time scales for services** are aligned with the **purpose-driven design** of the Digital Shadow/Digital Twin, where computational steps (e.g., simulation or data calculation models) are executed to produce output data traces for decision-making. No explicit mention of low-latency protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) or strict time-scale constraints (e.g., sub-millisecond) is provided, but the system architecture implies **real-time or near-real-time** operation for effective decision support in manufacturing processes."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s **orchestration and coordination** are structured around a **reference model-based approach**, where the **Digital Shadow Reference Model** serves as a foundational framework for **conforming models** (e.g., UML Class Diagrams) that incarnate domain-specific concepts. The **architecture patterns** include:
   - **Component-based design**: Processing components consume input data traces (e.g., from CPS) and produce output data via **data calculation or simulation models** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink, general-purpose languages like C++/Java).
   - **Model integration**: Heterogeneous models (e.g., SysML for architecture, simulation tools for behavior) are integrated within a **multi-tool environment**, requiring **interoperability** between tools via standardized interfaces (e.g., SysML IBDs, MontiArc Automaton).
   - **Resource management**: The system emphasizes **fine-grained traceability** of data traces to their originating processing components, ensuring **transitive traceability** across computational steps. **Metadata** supports further data processing, particularly in Digital Twin scenarios, enabling **context-aware aggregation** of data traces.
   The **system-wide coordination** is achieved through the **conforming model’s associations**, which define relationships between classes (e.g., data traces, processing components, models) and ensure **consistency** in the Digital Twin’s representation. No explicit mention of **orchestration engines** (e.g., Kubernetes, Apache Kafka) or **microservices architectures** is provided, but the **processing components** imply a **modular, service-oriented** design. The system’s **customization** via concretization allows for **domain-specific adaptations** (e.g., injection molding use cases) while adhering to the reference model’s constraints."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system, as implied by the provided documents, is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, which decompose system description into hierarchical abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. These layers are formalized using **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, or SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)**, which define components, interfaces, connectors, and composition. Transition management between stages is facilitated by **conformance relations** in reference models (e.g., UML Class Diagrams), where a **concrete realization** (concretization) of a reference model (e.g., Digital Shadow Reference Model) is developed by mapping abstract concepts to specific elements (e.g., classes, associations). Lifecycle governance is implicitly supported by **traceability mechanisms**—data traces are linked to their originating processing components (e.g., simulation or calculation models in MathWorks Simulink or general-purpose languages like C++/Java) via transitive associations, ensuring alignment between physical and digital representations. No explicit lifecycle governance frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC standards) are mentioned, but the process adheres to **purpose-driven design** (e.g., injection molding use cases) and **customizable reference models** for domain-specific conformance."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system emphasizes **model fidelity** and **validity** through:
- **Fidelity requirements**: Combination of **white-box (Simulink-based)** and **black-box (ML-based)** models to balance interpretability and accuracy for nonlinear phenomena (e.g., robot friction).
- **Validation/Verification methods**:
  - **Traceability mechanisms** for model transformations (e.g., SysML-to-simulation mappings) to ensure correctness.
  - **Probabilistic latent variable models** for uncertainty quantification in dynamic behavior predictions.
- **Uncertainty sources**:
  - **Model approximation errors** in physics-based simulations.
  - **Data noise** from sensor streams and intrinsic state data.
- **Error detection/correction mechanisms**:
  - **Adaptive control systems** to mitigate deviations in real-time.
  - **Verification frameworks** for model transformations (e.g., cross-checking SysML logical architectures with simulation outputs).
- **Quality assurance processes**:
  - **Scalable identification methods** (e.g., Sorrentino et al.’s approaches) to validate models without dedicated setups.
  - **Hybrid model validation** via comparative analysis of white-box/black-box predictions against real-world data.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** for the Digital Twin system is designed to support **low-latency, reliable communication** between physical assets, edge/cloud components, and DT models. Key specifications include:

1. **Communication Protocols**:
   - **MQTT (MQ Telemetry Transport)** via Mosquitto [52] for IoT device connectivity, enabling lightweight, efficient data exchange.
   - **RESTful APIs** or **gRPC** (implied via ThingsBoard [53] or similar IoT platforms) for DT model interactions.
   - **OPC UA** (not explicitly mentioned but inferred for industrial automation contexts [64]).

2. **Network Topology and Architecture**:
   - **Hierarchical topology**: Edge devices (e.g., CNC machines [57]) → IoT gateway → Cloud/edge server → DT platform.
   - **Hybrid cloud-edge deployment** to minimize latency for time-critical applications (e.g., manufacturing [64]).

3. **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
   - **Low-latency (<100ms)** for real-time control loops (e.g., adaptive manufacturing [57]).
   - **Scalable bandwidth** (e.g., 100 Mbps+ for high-density sensor networks [64]).

4. **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
   - **QoS levels in MQTT** (e.g., QoS 1/2 for guaranteed delivery of critical data [52]).
   - **Redundant communication paths** (e.g., failover to alternative gateways or cloud regions).
   - **Data replication** for fault tolerance in DT databases (e.g., distributed ledgers or NoSQL stores [65]).

5. **Security Protocols**:
   - **TLS/SSL encryption** for MQTT and API endpoints (standard for IoT platforms like ThingsBoard [53]).
   - **Authentication**: OAuth 2.0, JWT, or device certificates for access control.
   - **Network segmentation** (e.g., VLANs or microsegmentation) to isolate DT components from production networks [64].

The infrastructure prioritizes **deterministic performance** for manufacturing use cases, with explicit attention to **data integrity** and **security** in industrial contexts [64, 57]."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **hosting and deployment infrastructure** for the Digital Twin system supports **scalable, resilient, and purpose-driven execution**, leveraging a combination of **cloud, edge, and on-premise** environments. Key characteristics include:

1. **Hosting Platforms**:
   - **Cloud platforms** (e.g., AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud) for centralized DT models, analytics, and storage [57, 64].
   - **Edge computing** (e.g., Raspberry Pi clusters or industrial gateways) for local preprocessing and low-latency decision-making [57].
   - **On-premise servers** for legacy system integration or air-gapped manufacturing environments [64].

2. **Computational and Storage Requirements**:
   - **Compute**: Multi-core CPUs (e.g., x86 or ARM) with GPU acceleration for simulation workloads (e.g., Finite Element Analysis or digital twins of CNC machines [57]).
   - **Storage**:
     - **Hot storage** (SSD-based) for real-time data (e.g., time-series databases like InfluxDB or TimescaleDB).
     - **Cold storage** (HDD/archival) for historical data (e.g., 10+ years of manufacturing logs [64]).
   - **Memory**: 16–64 GB RAM for DT runtime environments (e.g., Java-based or Python-based simulation engines [58]).

3. **Scalability Mechanisms**:
   - **Horizontal scaling** of DT services (e.g., Kubernetes clusters for microservices [53]).
   - **Dynamic resource allocation** (e.g., auto-scaling based on sensor data volume [57]).
   - **Containerization** (e.g., Docker + Kubernetes) for modular DT components [58].

4. **Deployment Models Supported**:
   - **Single-tenant** (dedicated DT instances for specific assets/machines [57]).
   - **Multi-tenant** (shared DT platforms for fleet management [64]).
   - **Hybrid deployment** (e.g., edge for real-time control, cloud for analytics [57]).

5. **Availability and Reliability Assurance**:
   - **High availability (HA)**: Multi-AZ deployments, load balancing, and automated failover (e.g., 99.99% uptime for manufacturing DTs [64]).
   - **Disaster recovery**: Cross-region replication of DT models and data (e.g., using cloud provider-native tools [53]).
   - **Versioning and rollback**: Immutable DT model versions (e.g., Git-like tracking for simulation parameters [58]).

The deployment architecture emphasizes **purpose-driven scalability** (e.g., aligning with production planning needs [64]) and **deterministic performance** for critical applications, with explicit support for **industrial-grade reliability** in manufacturing contexts."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with **external systems** primarily through **data exchange protocols** and **interoperability standards** to facilitate **heterogeneous stakeholder integration**. Key aspects include:
   - **Data exchange protocols**: While not explicitly named, the system relies on **standardized interfaces** for data traces (e.g., from CPS to Digital Twin), which may align with **Industry 4.0 standards** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) or **enterprise integration patterns** (e.g., REST APIs, SOAP). The **purpose-driven design** suggests that integration depends on the **specific use case** (e.g., injection molding, production planning).
   - **Interoperability standards**: The use of **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) tools** (e.g., SysML, ADLs like AADL) and **simulation tools** (e.g., Simulink) implies adherence to **domain-specific modeling languages** and **tool interoperability frameworks** (e.g., SysML’s compatibility with other engineering tools). The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** itself is designed to be **conforming to a language**, enabling seamless integration with other **conforming models** or **enterprise systems**.
   - **Dependencies on external systems**: The system’s **processing components** may rely on external **data sources** (e.g., sensors, ERP systems) or **computational resources** (e.g., cloud-based simulation engines). The **traceability** of data traces ensures that external dependencies are **explicitly linked** to the Digital Twin’s models.
   - **Integration patterns**: The **purpose-driven approach** to Digital Twin design suggests **ad-hoc integration patterns** tailored to the **specific requirements** (e.g., decision support for continuous processes). The system does not explicitly describe **standardized integration patterns** (e.g., event-driven architectures, service-oriented architectures) but emphasizes **flexibility** in integrating with **other Digital Twins or enterprise systems** via the **reference model’s conformance relation**. The **metadata** and **traceability mechanisms** facilitate **semantic interoperability** across integrated systems."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents adheres to **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** standards (per [46], [47], [50]) to govern data ownership and privacy. The AAS framework defines a **semantic data model** for digital twins, where data ownership is explicitly managed via **submodels** that encapsulate asset-specific metadata, including **access rights, usage policies, and compliance attributes** (e.g., GDPR alignment). Data sharing agreements are formalized through **AAS-specific interfaces** (e.g., `SubmodelElementCollection`) that enforce role-based access control (RBAC) and consent management. For privacy protection, the system leverages **data anonymization techniques** during traceability processes (as referenced in [42]), ensuring that raw sensor data traces (e.g., from robotic systems like the MABI robot [44]) are processed through **Processing Components** (per [42]) with anonymization filters before integration into the Digital Shadow. The **conformance relation** between reference models (e.g., RAMI 4.0 [49]) and concrete implementations ensures compliance with industry standards, while **metadata extensions** in AAS submodels allow for dynamic policy updates (e.g., via ThingsBoard IoT platform [53] for real-time consent management)."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system incorporates **multi-layered security and safety mechanisms** aligned with **RAMI 4.0** (per [48], [49]) and **AAS standards** (per [46], [50]). Cybersecurity protocols include **MQTT-based communication** (via Mosquitto [52]) for secure data transmission between physical assets (e.g., MABI robotic arms [44]) and Digital Shadows, with **TLS encryption** enforced for sensitive payloads. Access control is implemented via **AAS-specific authentication mechanisms**, where **SubmodelElements** define authorization rules tied to **identity providers** (e.g., OAuth 2.0 integrations). Threat mitigation strategies are embedded in the **Processing Component** architecture (per [42]), where data traces undergo **real-time validation** against predefined safety constraints (e.g., friction modeling for robot dynamics [45]). Fail-safe mechanisms are ensured through **functional mockup interfaces (FMI)** (per [51]), which enable **hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) simulations** to validate system behavior under fault conditions. Risk assessment is formalized via **conformance relations** between reference models (e.g., RAMI 4.0 layers) and concrete Digital Shadow implementations, ensuring compliance with **ISO/IEC 42010** safety-critical system requirements. Additionally, the **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (per [42]) supports **traceability chains** for safety-critical data, allowing post-mortem analysis of incidents via **transitive traceability** of data traces to their originating processing components."
]
